signal.headline: Liberation Day tariffs confirm worst-case trade scenario; private credit gate events trigger at Ares and Apollo; consumer confidence breaks below GFC trough

debt_dynamics.reading: $38T debt; FY2026 deficit tracking ~$1.9T (7.2% GDP); US refinancing $10T this year
debt_dynamics.update_this_week: OECD Global Debt Report 2026: US accounts for 70% of OECD refinancing requirements ($9.5T of $13.5T total), up from 57% in 2020. IMF Article IV warns general government debt reaches 140% GDP by 2030.

debt_dynamics.reading: 30yr JGB above 3.5%; BoJ pain threshold breached; ~¥400T life insurer/pension holdings at risk
debt_dynamics.update_this_week: Unchanged from prior week — persistent stress.

debt_dynamics.reading: EMBI spreads widening; Fragile Five individually stressed
debt_dynamics.update_this_week: Liberation Day tariffs add EM stress: Asia facing heaviest impact (China 34%, other Asian economies similarly hit). Coface estimates average effective rate 26.2%.

credit_stress.reading: Gate events confirmed at Ares (11.6% redemption requests, restricted to 5%), Apollo, Blackstone BCRED (first monthly loss in 3 years, Feb -0.4%)
credit_stress.update_this_week: MATERIAL ESCALATION: Ares Management restricted A Strategic Income fund redemptions to 5% after withdrawal requests hit 11.6% (Bloomberg, March 25). Apollo implemented similar restrictions. Blackstone BCRED marked down loans including Medallia (SaaS). Morgan Stanley maintains 8% default rate forecast for direct lending. Goldman Sachs (Vivek Bantwal) argues fundamentals strong but acknowledges retail outflow pressure is technical stress. CNBC: this is the first substantial liquidity test for private credit 'at scale'.

credit_stress.reading: CMBS office 12.34% — all-time high, approaching GFC peak (~13%)

credit_stress.update_this_week: No new SLOOS data this week.

systemic_risk.reading: Liberation Day April 2: 10%+ reciprocal tariffs on all trading partners; China 34%, EU 20%, Japan 24%; average effective rate 26.2% — highest since Smoot-Hawley 1930
systemic_risk.update_this_week: MAJOR ESCALATION: Trump announced 'reciprocal' tariffs on all countries on April 2 ('Liberation Day'), effective April 5 for base 10%, April 9 for higher rates. China 34%, EU 20%, Japan 24%. Coface: average effective rate 26.2% vs 2.3% in 2024 — most abrupt change since Smoot-Hawley. Canada retaliating with 25% on US cars. EU Anti-Coercion Instrument possible. April 3 auto tariffs (25%) already in force. April 9 CIT court hearing on Section 122 authority is the primary reversal catalyst.

systemic_risk.reading: Brent peaked $126/bbl; EIA Q2 2026 avg forecast $91/bbl; Hormuz flows at ~4M bpd (vs normal 16M); Gulf producers cutting 10M bpd
systemic_risk.current_driver: Iran conflict / Hormuz restriction — largest supply disruption in history of global oil market
systemic_risk.update_this_week: MATERIAL ESCALATION since March 24 baseline: Brent hit $126/bbl peak (intellectia.ai, March 23), Dubai crude $166.80/bbl. JPMorgan: Hormuz flows plummeted to ~4M bpd from normal 16M. EIA STEO raised 2026 Brent forecast to $78.84/bbl avg (was $57.69), Q2 avg ~$91/bbl. Goldman Sachs $18/bbl risk premium. Fed March FOMC explicitly cited Iran conflict as clouding policy path. EIA: shut-in production peaks early April, mainly Iraq.

systemic_risk.reading: UMich final March 2026: 53.3 — revised DOWN from 55.5 preliminary; below GFC November 2008 trough (55.3)
systemic_risk.update_this_week: MATERIAL DETERIORATION: Final reading revised to 53.3 (Chronicle-Journal/Trading Economics, March 30–31). This is the 2026 low and below the November 2008 GFC trough (55.3) — previously our crisis threshold. 'Soft landing is in jeopardy; inflation expectations are unmooring' (Chronicle-Journal). Conference Board March index only 91.8 — Expectations sub-index at 70.9 (below 80 = recession signal historically).

systemic_risk.reading: Fed held at 3.50–3.75% March 18; upgraded 2026 inflation forecast to 2.7% PCE; market now pricing zero cuts in 2026 (vs two cuts priced at start of year)
systemic_risk.update_this_week: Fed March 18 meeting: held rates, upgraded PCE forecast to 2.7%, longer-run rate raised to 3.1%. Powell 'wait-and-see' on Iran impact. FedWatch: market pricing ~4% probability of hike at April meeting, 0% cut. JPMorgan Chase strategists: one 0.25% cut still expected by year-end. IMF Article IV (Feb): baseline calls for 3.25–3.50% by end 2026. Policy trap deepening: Hormuz oil shock adds inflationary pressure exactly when consumer confidence breaks GFC trough.

asset_outlook.key_driver: Consumer confidence 53.3 — now below GFC trough. Stagflation regime destroys defensive trade. Liberation Day tariffs add cost-push on staples.
oil_supply_shock (50% weight) RED and escalating. Brent $126 peak; EIA Q2 avg $91/bbl. Liberation Day tariffs add demand-destruction risk.
CRE delinquency 12.34% all-time high. Private credit gate events confirmed — removes the primary refinancing bridge for CRE.
FX swap basis improving (GREEN) partially offsets tariff and oil shock. BUT Liberation Day tariffs hit Asian EMs hardest — this improvement may be temporary.
asset_outlook.tactical_note: Score improvement of +0.19 is the largest single-week move but does NOT trigger WATCH (threshold 0.45). Driven by fx_swap_basis improving direction. Monitor April 9 for tariff escalation response.

asset_outlook.key_driver: Nominal M2 +4.88% narrative thinning in real terms. Blind Spot Rule 2 active.
Near-term: dollar safe-haven bid compresses gold. Secular: EM CB accumulation, dollar weaponisation, debt trajectory all intact. Stagflation #1 ranked asset class historically.
JGB spiral risk; inflation above target prevents duration extension. Fed policy trap: cannot cut into $91/bbl oil.
Q1 earnings season begins this week — first test of tariff/AI capex margin assumptions. Blind Spot Rule 1 active: earnings_revisions GREEN may be suppressed.

safe_haven.rationale: Stagflation historically places Gold as the #1 ranked asset class (Robeco 146-year study). Secular drivers intact: EM CB gold accumulation accelerating, dollar reserve share erosion, US debt/deficit trajectory. Near-term score (-0.446) reflects dollar safe-haven compression but the structural bid is building, not fading. Liberation Day tariffs add a new inflationary impulse that benefits real assets over nominal bonds.
safe_haven.condition_for_reassessment: Core PCE below 2.5% sustained (regime shift to Deflationary Bust) → rotate to government bonds. Real M2 turning negative Q3 2026 would accelerate deflationary bust probability (currently 25%).
safe_haven.not_investment_advice: This is a model output based on indicator flags, regime classification, and historical backtesting. It is not investment advice. Consult a qualified financial adviser before making investment decisions.

flags.title: Hyperscaler capex trajectory reinforces AI Infrastructure Debt indicator
flags.body: UPDATED: OpenAI closed additional $12B tranche, bringing total to $122B at $852B valuation (AI Governance Monitor W/E 1 April 2026). Gimlet Labs $80M (Aramco hedging energy disruption via AI infrastructure). This directly reinforces ai_infra_debt (ELEVATED). Late-cycle AI valuations funded against tightening liquidity backdrop: private credit gate events now active (Ares, Apollo), creating contradiction — AI funding requires cheap capital exactly when private credit is gating redemptions. Energy Wall regulatory development: EIA pilot survey (mandatory reporting precursor) and Ratepayer Protection Pledge position hyperscalers as regulated utilities — adds structural compliance cost that was not in prior capex models. AI capex is both a debt amplifier (2026-2027 risk) and potential deflationary force (2028-2030).

flags.title: AI labour displacement beginning to show in entry-level employment data
flags.body: UPGRADED: NBER Working Paper 34836 (cited in AI Governance Monitor W/E 1 April 2026): 502,000 AI-driven job cuts projected in 2026 — nine times the 55,000 in 2025. Duke CFO survey corroborates. Quinnipiac (30 March): 70% of Americans expect job losses from AI. Critical dynamic: 90% of senior executives report zero past employment impact from AI, yet the same executives project 9x acceleration in 2026. This non-linear disruption curve is the most dangerous data point for the Macro Monitor's jobless_claims and consumer_confidence indicators — if realised, it would push both RED and accelerate the Consumer Staples BEARISH score further. The Fed cannot model this: AI displacement masking beneath aggregate labour statistics makes their labour market anchor even weaker than the payrolls/claims divergence already suggests.

flags.title: EU CSRD Phase 2 creates mandatory climate risk disclosure for financial institutions
flags.body: CSRD Phase 2 entered force 1 January 2026; first reporting obligations 2027. Double materiality requirements expose EU financial institutions. Structural cost amplifier for G-SIB institutions already under capital pressure.

flags.title: Iran/Hormuz conflict drives largest oil supply disruption in history — directly drives oil_supply_shock RED escalation
flags.body: Hormuz flows at ~4M bpd (vs normal 16M bpd). Gulf producers cutting 10M bpd. Brent peaked $126/bbl. This is now the dominant near-term driver of the Macro Monitor's Energy score (-0.785 BEARISH) and a primary input into Consumer Staples and EM Equities. Sudan economic warfare indicator also above baseline — reinforces em_sovereign_distress. Myanmar junta Iranian jet fuel supply is secondary Hormuz signal.

flags.title: No material macro-financial cross-signal identified this period
flags.body: FIMI operations focused on electoral interference. No identified macro-financial market manipulation. Hungary election (April 12) remains a speculative EM stress catalyst for HUF.

flags.title: Hungary election April 12 — potential EM stress catalyst for HUF and Hungarian sovereign spreads
flags.body: WATCH MAINTAINED — 11 days to Hungary election (April 12). PolitPro aggregate (31 March): Tisza 50.8%, Fidesz 47.8%. TVP World: Tisza pulling ahead in Orbán strongholds for first time. Structural constraint: electoral redistricting requires Tisza to outperform by 3-5pp nationally for parliamentary majority. Polling divergence: independent pollsters give Tisza 10+ point lead; MAGA-aligned McLaughlin & Associates gives Fidesz 10 point lead. FIMI Monitor: Storm-1516 deploying Euronews impersonation — highest documented pre-election FIMI density for a single EU member state. Macro relevance: A Tisza victory and subsequent Russian economic retaliation could spike HUF and Hungarian sovereign spreads — relevant to em_sovereign_distress. No market impact yet — outcome unknown until April 12.

flags.title: No material macro-financial cross-signal identified this period
flags.body: NATO 20% European defence spending increase is a structural fiscal signal. Liberation Day tariffs (EU 20%) add a direct trade shock to European economies — this is now captured in trump_tariffs indicator directly.

version_history.change: Initial population — 7 cross-monitor flags from inaugural scan
version_history.reason: First run of cross-monitor scan protocol

version_history.change: cmf_004 updated: Hormuz now confirmed as largest supply disruption in history; directly drives Energy score escalation. cmf_006 upgraded from NoSignal to Watch: Hungary election April 12 approaching.
version_history.reason: Hormuz disruption materially escalated per EIA, JPMorgan, Reuters data. Hungary election 13 days away.

version_history.change: cmf_001 updated: OpenAI $852B/$122B total; Energy Wall regulatory developments. cmf_002 upgraded to STRUCTURAL: NBER WP 34836 projects 502K AI job cuts in 2026 (9x 2025). cmf_006 Watch maintained: Tisza 50.8% with 11 days to election; FIMI at maximum pre-election density.
version_history.reason: Scheduled Wednesday cron run #2 — AI Governance W/E 1 April 2026 brief; Democratic Integrity W/E 1 April 2026 brief; FIMI W/E 1 April 2026 brief.

executive_briefing.summary: Liberation Day tariffs confirm worst-case trade scenario; private credit gate events trigger at Ares and Apollo; consumer confidence breaks below GFC trough
executive_briefing.one_number_to_watch: VIX elevated with CDX at 9-month high while S&P within 5% of all-time highs — this combination preceded drawdowns in 2007, 2015, and 2022 every single time.
executive_briefing.one_indicator_lying: Nominal M2 growth (+4.88%). Real M2 vs. Core PCE is +1.78% and narrowing. The market is using the nominal figure to justify compressed risk premia.

real_m2.note: Real M2 vs. Core PCE +1.78% and narrowing — nominal M2 (+4.88%) masks the compression in real liquidity growth; vs. Core PPI only +0.98%, signalling the transmission mechanism is nearly exhausted

hard_landing_risk.note: Private credit gate events at Ares/Apollo, consumer confidence below GFC trough, and tariff shock compressing forward earnings raise hard landing probability materially above baseline

scenario_analysis.label: Base Case
scenario_analysis.name: Slow Burn
scenario_analysis.description: Tariff shock absorbed gradually; Fed on hold; credit stress contained to private markets; S&P range-bound -10% to +5%; stagflation regime persists

scenario_analysis.label: De-escalation
scenario_analysis.name: Policy Pivot
scenario_analysis.description: Trade deal framework or Fed pivot signal triggers relief rally; credit spreads compress; regime transitions from STAGFLATION toward RISK-ON; equities +8–15%

scenario_analysis.label: Fast Cascade
scenario_analysis.name: Credit Contagion
scenario_analysis.description: Private credit gate events escalate to CLO and IG contagion; VIX spikes above 40; hard landing probability surges; Fed forced to emergency cut; equities -20%+

tail_risks.label: Private Credit Cascade
tail_risks.note: [Near] Gate events at Ares/Apollo signal retail outflow pressure; CLO and IG credit contagion if redemption restrictions spread to additional funds.

tail_risks.label: Tariff Stagflation Lock
tail_risks.note: [Near] Liberation Day 26.2% effective rate now highest since Smoot-Hawley 1930; cost-push across 7 of 8 asset classes with no Fed policy escape.

tail_risks.label: Consumer Demand Cliff
tail_risks.note: [Near] Consumer confidence 53.3 — below GFC trough; if spending contracts sharply, stagflation transitions to recession regime.

tail_risks.label: JGB Yield Spiral
tail_risks.note: [Medium] BoJ forced to choose between FX defence and yield cap; unwind of $4T carry trade has no modern precedent for orderly resolution.

tail_risks.label: VIX/Credit Snap
tail_risks.note: [Near] CDX at 9-month high while S&P within 5% of ATH — compression preceded drawdowns in 2007, 2015, and 2022 every time.

tail_risks.label: US Refinancing Shock
tail_risks.note: [Medium] $10T US debt refinancing in 2026 at elevated rates; 70% of OECD refinancing requirement concentrated in one sovereign.

fed_funds_futures.note: Hold certain — Liberation Day tariffs eliminate any near-term cut window
Unlikely — stagflation regime prevents pre-emptive cut
Market pricing too aggressive — inflation re-acceleration risk from tariff pass-through
Only if hard landing materialises; stagflation base case precludes
Year-end cumulative cuts priced too high; Macquarie base case is a hike in H1 2027
